proposal.reuseVsNew:
REUSED VERBATIM (no edit): TRUST_META, TRUST_TAB_ORDER, StatusChip, splitLead, SectionEyebrow, HairRule, ArrowRight, DraftChip, usePrefersReducedMotion, the editorial-link sweep + bench-row entrance <style>, the tile-indigo ordinal badge, the entire PR1 single-column trust index and BenchmarksEmptyState, the .trend-detail-enter fade, BackLink/Beat/Prose/MicroLabel/ExampleTask/WatchOut/GoDeeper, HonestEmpty, the PR1 routes' force-dynamic + await-params + redirect-on-miss shape, the asLeaders/asStringArray/asRawRelatedConcepts parsers and the concept-catalog validation, the seed-script validator + BANNED_DASHES sweep, the admin requireAdmin + publish/publishAll PATCH pattern, the Benchmark model's inline-Json + draft-gate shape, the sidebar item (prefix-match already covers /benchmarks/use/*).

REUSED WITH A MECHANICAL CHANGE: BenchmarkRow (lifted/imported to render the use-case yardsticks and adapted into UseCaseRow with a backing-chips cell); ScoreStamp + FlagChip + monYear + domainOf (exported from benchmark-detail-client, no behavior change) so HonestPick welds the identical receipt; BenchmarkModeration (copied to UseCaseModeration pointed at the new route); BenchmarksClient (one additive prop + one block above existing content in Option A).

GENUINELY NEW (kept minimal): one Json column (Benchmark.useCases) + one Prisma model (UseCase); getUseCases/getUseCaseDetail; one seed file + one seed script + one admin route; one page route + one client (UseCaseDetailClient, itself ~80% reused primitives); the one new presentational component HonestPick + its EmptyPick branch + a PICK_VERB lookup + a SCAFFOLD FlagChip variant. No new token, no new card system, no new motion, no edit to the trust spine.
topRisks: Pick receipts drifting from the source board: an authored honestPick could cite a model/score that no longer matches the benchmark's leaders Json. Mitigation: getUseCaseDetail RESOLVES each pick's receipt from the source benchmark's leaders at render time (single source of truth), and seed --verify rejects a pick whose benchmarkSlug/leaderRef does not resolve.

Lifting BenchmarkRow for reuse: it is module-local in benchmarks-client.tsx, so importing it for the use-case page means exporting it (a small surface change) or duplicating it. Mitigation: export it (preferred, no duplication) and keep its <style> dependencies (bench-row keyframes) co-located or copied into the consuming component's <style>.

A second TRUST/order copy: PICK_VERB risks becoming a third parallel definition beside TRUST_ORDER (lib) and TRUST_TAB_ORDER (client). Mitigation: define PICK_VERB once beside TRUST_META and key it off the same trust strings; do not re-encode order.

Two-surface honest-empty fatigue: with 6 honest-empty + 2 self-reported boards, many use cases look thin, risking a 'broken site' read for non-technical students. Mitigation: keep the four states visually/verbally distinct (stone DATED vs steel SELF-REPORTED vs gold SCAFFOLD vs truly-empty prose), and frame thinness as the lesson in copy, never as a stub or coming-soon.

Option A page length: stacking the explorer above The Standings makes /benchmarks long. Mitigation: keep UseCaseRows compact (one audience line, chips only) and offer Option B (separate /benchmarks/use index) if the combined page tests too heavy; both keep PR1 untouched.

Prisma client HMR after schema change: adding useCases + UseCase will 500 with 'Cannot read properties of undefined' until the dev server is restarted post db push/generate (known gotcha). Mitigation: rm .next, restart dev on 3100, verify title reads 'AISA Atlas' before testing.

Scaffold/metric caveats getting skipped by authors: the SCAFFOLD and metric-disagreement notes are the most important new caveats (OSWorld, EQ-Bench) and are author-entered. Mitigation: make them structural fields on the honestPick shape (scaffoldNote?, metricNote?) and surface them in the pick caveat slot so an author fills them deliberately, with a seed --verify nudge.

attacks.verdict: KEEP the spine, FIX the over-build, KILL the default-mode flip
attacks.trustIntegrity: Strong, arguably the strongest of the three. A model name physically cannot appear on the explorer (col-3 is StatusChip pills only), the PickStamp welds claim to receipt, the trust-gated PICK_VERB lookup makes the verb a deterministic function of tier, and there is no rollup anywhere. The one latent leak it correctly names in its own risks is a backdoor composite via explorer ordering, and its mitigation (order by the USE CASE live-backing count, never any model) holds. No averaging, no catalog, no fabricated leader. This is honest.
attacks.audienceFit: Good but heavier than it admits. The hairline col-3 'evidence readout' (a row of sm StatusChips plus a computed '3 boards, 1 live' gloss) is genuinely scannable and is the best pre-click honesty signal of any proposal. The risk is cognitive: a mixed-background student lands on a wall of trust-tier chips for tasks they have not chosen yet, which is the literacy lesson but not the fast decision they came for. The two-column use-case page with the right-rail UseCasePickPanel is correct and reuses LeaderPanel rhythm cleanly.
attacks.honestEmptyReality: Best-in-class. Three distinct states (honest-empty / self-reported-only / dated) kept verbally and chromatically separate, full-weight HonestEmpty body reused verbatim, a labeled 'Tasks no benchmark measures cleanly yet' grouped section so 6 empties read as a deliberate section not 6 holes. This is exactly how the 6-empty-plus-2-self-reported reality should be carried.
attacks.buildability: Buildable but the largest export-refactor surface of the three: it exports ScoreStamp, FlagChip, monYear, domainOf AND Beat, Prose, MicroLabel, ExampleTask, BackLink, GoDeeper, WatchOut from benchmark-detail-client, plus CategoryTab from benchmarks-client, plus a new BenchmarksHub wrapper, plus two new client components. Every one of these is confirmed module-local today (verified: only StatusChip is exported), so each export is real churn against the PR1 detail file the brief says not to destabilize. No new chrome, no new token, which is correct. The framework-fork risk (AGENTS.md: this is NOT the Next.js you know, read node_modules/next/dist/docs) applies to its new /benchmarks/use/[slug] route exactly as to mine.
attacks.scope: Honestly the heaviest of the four on front-end churn while claiming minimalism. The PR2a/b/c phasing is reviewable, but PR2b introduces a NEW two-mode hub toggle and makes 'By use case' the DEFAULT landing of /benchmarks. That default flip is the one thing to KILL or defer: it silently demotes The Standings, the proven PR1 surface, behind a tab a returning user must now hunt for, and it is the single change most likely to regress trust-first muscle memory. FIX: keep The Standings as the landing, add the explorer as a block or a second clearly-marked door, do not make the task grid the default. The rest is keep.

attacks.verdict: FIX heavily, KILL the card grid as the explorer idiom
attacks.trustIntegrity: Mostly honest at the pick level (welded ScoreStamp, trust-gated verb, SCAFFOLD chip, self-reported conflict caveat all present) but the card grid is the riskiest trust surface of any proposal. A repeat(2,1fr) grid of equal bordered cards is the exact league-table read the PR1 panel rejected one level down. The author knows this and lists it as risk #1 with mitigations (never number, order editorially, withhold scores and model names, chip-mix as the only signal). Those mitigations help but do not fully remove the read: equal cells in a grid still imply comparable, ranked peers in a way a single hairline column does not. This is the one place a composite-shaped impression can sneak in through layout alone, with no composite in the data.
attacks.audienceFit: Highest immediate recognizability for a mixed-background student because it borrows benchlm's proven information design, and the dated bottom-line plus decision-ready picks plus how-to-choose blocks map cleanly to how a non-technical person reads. That familiarity is a genuine strength and the reason to keep the use-case PAGE largely as designed. The cost is that the recognizable surface is also the one that imports benchlm's confident scoreboard affect, against the trust-first voice.
attacks.honestEmptyReality: Handled well on the page (zero picks, why-empty prose, dated anchor, four distinct states) but WEAKEST on the explorer of the three honest stances. An honest-empty use case rendered as a full-size card with a '1 board, 0 live' line still occupies a confident card slot identical to a populated one; thinness inside a uniform card reads more like 'not loaded yet' than the deliberate authored gap a hairline row with a stone DATED chip and the words 'No live board' conveys. With 6 empties this matters: the card format fights the honesty.
attacks.buildability: Introduces the one new CSS class of any proposal, .usecase-grid (repeat(2,minmax(0,1fr)) gap 16), which is new chrome the brief asked us to justify against the editorial voice. It is honestly flagged and built from the validated Brilliant bordered-card aesthetic with no shadow, so it is not egregious, but it is a new card system on a surface that PR1 deliberately kept cardless. Same export-then-reuse refactor as the others; same framework-fork route risk. The pick/leader DATA DUPLICATION (authoring picks[] separately from Benchmark.leaders) is its real buildability weakness: the author concedes the two can drift and only a future PR derives picks from leaders. That is the exact dishonesty this page exists to prevent, shipped as a known debt.
attacks.scope: Four PRs (A data, B page, C explorer, D Playwright), reviewable, but it carries the new CSS class, the data-duplication debt, and the most affect-laden surface. FIX: drop the card grid, render the explorer as the hairline contents-page row idiom (keep everything else, the page is good); resolve picks from the source leaders at render time instead of duplicating, which removes risk #2 entirely.

attacks.verdict: KEEP the decision spine and the page, FIX the route split, soften the landing
attacks.trustIntegrity: Honest. No composite, no catalog, picks are the welded ScoreStamp atom, VERB_BY_TRUST gates loudness by tier, the rail shows 'As of MON YYYY' rather than crowning an overall winner, and Playwright asserts no 'overall' element. The decomposition-not-averaging framing (decompose the goal, let the sub-goal select one board) is the cleanest articulation of the sanctioned escape hatch from the locked-out composite of any proposal. The judge-it-yourself reframe for honest-empty introduces zero invented numbers.
attacks.audienceFit: Strongest decision ergonomics for the target student: the page opens with a question, walks task to yardsticks to trust to one receipted pick or a stated reason, and the honest-empty reframe ('we are not naming a winner, judge it yourself on these two things') converts a dead end into a method. This is the most genuinely useful arc for someone who arrived with a task and no vocabulary. The how-to-choose-as-spine choice (not a sidebar) is correct for this audience.
attacks.honestEmptyReality: Excellent. The judge-it-yourself decision aid plus the dated anchor in the rail plus visible thinness on the explorer is a first-class treatment of the common case, equal to the editorial-minimalist's and arguably warmer for a non-technical reader because it hands them a method instead of only a labeled absence.
attacks.buildability: Buildable with the same export refactor (ScoreStamp/FlagChip/monYear/domainOf, all confirmed module-local), the hairline explorer (no new chrome, correctly rejects the card grid), and the existing trend-detail-split for the walk. The single-column use-case page (deliberately NOT two-column, to avoid re-implying a league table) is a sound call and reuses primitives. Same framework-fork route caution.
attacks.scope: The most aggressive routing change of the four, and that is the thing to FIX. It MOVES The Standings to a new /benchmarks/all route and makes the decision explorer the /benchmarks landing. That is a five-PR effort and it relocates the proven PR1 surface, which is more rework and more regression surface than the brief needs. It also means every existing link, bookmark, or sidebar expectation pointing at /benchmarks now lands on the explorer, not the trust list. FIX: do not relocate The Standings; layer the explorer in (a block above, or a sibling door) and keep /benchmarks pointing at the trust-first list, or at minimum keep a prominent in-place path to it. Keep the page, keep the spine, keep the honest-empty reframe; just stop moving the foundation.

ideasToKeep: The hairline contents-page row idiom for the explorer (single column, BenchmarkRow grammar reused verbatim, position-only tile-indigo glyph, no model name and no score ever on the surface). Three of four proposals converge here, and it is correct: it enforces honest-picks-only at the architecture level because there is no surface where a bare best-model string can render.

Backing-benchmarks-as-StatusChips on each explorer row (editorial-minimalist's 'evidence readout' and the others' backing-chip strip). This makes honesty density visible BEFORE the click and turns thinness into a scannable warning, not a post-click disappointment. Pair it with a plain '3 boards, 1 live' / 'No live board' text gloss so trust is never color-only.

The welded PickStamp/HonestPick built by wrapping the EXPORTED ScoreStamp atom, so the flattering half (model name) is physically un-screenshot-able without the qualifying half (date, source, flags). Confirmed feasible: ScoreStamp/FlagChip/monYear/domainOf are module-local today and only need exporting, no signature change.

The trust-tier-gated pick verb lookup (PICK_VERB / VERB_BY_TRUST) keyed off the same trust strings as TRUST_META: live tops, near_ceiling leads-within-noise, contested/self-reported claims, dated led-as-of past tense, near-tie demotes to co-leaders. Define it ONCE beside TRUST_META, do not re-encode order.

The SCAFFOLD FlagChip variant for OSWorld, implemented by passing an existing tile pair (gold or steel) to the already-generic FlagChip(label, tile) signature, plus a mandatory 'what was tested' disclosure line. No new token needed; the chip vocabulary just gains a third word.

Metric-disagreement-as-two-branches (EQ-Bench Elo vs Rubric become two separate how-to-choose sub-goals landing on different picks) rather than collapsing into one false winner. All proposals adopt this; it is the honest move.

The decision-flow-first honest-empty reframe: 'we are not naming a winner, judge it yourself on these two things' plus the dated anchor in the rail. This is the warmest, most actionable handling of the common zero-or-one-live-leader case for a non-technical student.

The four-state visual/verbal discipline (live / dated / self-reported-contested / truly-empty) kept distinct using only the existing tier palette, to prevent caveat-fatigue grey. Editorial-minimalist and I both insist on this; it is essential given 6 empties plus 2 self-reported.

Picks RESOLVED from the source benchmark's leaders Json at render time (my reuse-maximalist mechanic), so an authored pick can never drift out of sync with the board it cites. This directly removes the benchlm proposal's conceded data-duplication debt and the staleness risk that is exactly what this page exists to flag.

UseCase as a real Prisma model mirroring Benchmark's draft-gate plus inline-Json shape, so it inherits the publish path, the BANNED_DASHES sweep, the @@index([status]) visibility rule, and a near-copy admin route and Moderation card for free. All four converge here; it is the lowest-invention content layer.

relatedConcepts-style live validation of every benchmarkSlug a use case references, so a draft or deleted board drops its chip/receipt rather than leaking. Reuses the exact pattern already in lib/benchmarks.ts.
ideasToKill: The benchlm 4-column (or 2-column) card grid as the explorer idiom, including the new .usecase-grid CSS class. It is the one new card system on a surface PR1 deliberately kept cardless, it is new chrome, and equal cells in a grid re-imply ranked comparable peers, the exact league-table read the PR1 panel rejected. Kill the grid; keep the hairline rows.

Making 'By use case' the DEFAULT landing mode of /benchmarks (editorial-minimalist's hub toggle default). This silently demotes The Standings, the proven trust-first surface, behind a tab. Violates the spirit of 'do not redesign the trust spine' by hiding it. Keep The Standings as the landing or a co-equal clearly-marked door.

Relocating The Standings to a new /benchmarks/all route and repointing /benchmarks at the explorer (decision-flow-first). This MOVES the proven PR1 foundation, breaks every existing /benchmarks expectation, and is more rework and regression surface than the brief needs. Layer the explorer in; do not move the foundation.

Authoring honestPicks as standalone score/date/model strings DECOUPLED from Benchmark.leaders (benchlm proposal's conceded duplication). A pick that can drift from its source board is precisely the dishonesty this page exists to prevent. Resolve picks from the source leaders at render time instead.

Any explorer ordering, tally, or summary keyed off a MODEL (a 'tops N of 11' count, a most-picked-model rollup, sorting by a model win count). This is the locked-out cross-benchmark composite wearing a new hat. Explorer order must be by the USE CASE live-backing count only, and no model name may appear on the explorer at all.

The two-column trend-detail-split with a leader RAIL on the use-case page IF it is used to stack a 1-2-3 podium or a vs-#N delta per task (decision-flow-first correctly avoids this; flagging it as a trap). A per-task podium re-imports the league-table the page rejects. Picks must be a hairline-separated column with no medal and no rank delta.
Numbering the use cases as ranks (any glyph that reads 01 beats 02). Position-only badges or task glyphs only; ordering is editorial and explicitly never a quality rank.

Any price / speed / latency / context-window branch in how-to-choose, or any Cheapest / Fastest / Largest-context pick. These require the locked-out model catalog. Branches split only on task shape a benchmark actually measures.
critique.bestExplorerIdiom: A single-column editorial HAIRLINE index of use cases at --maxw-content, reusing the PR1 BenchmarkRow grammar verbatim (position-only tile-indigo glyph, task name as hero at --text-xl with the editorial-link sweep, a one-line plain-language audienceLine in --color-text-2, full-row absolute Link to /benchmarks/use/[slug], inheriting the bench-row entrance and the <=600px collapse for free). The right cell replaces PR1's single STATUS column with the backing-benchmarks-as-StatusChips strip: each of the 2-to-3 ordered backing boards rendered as its sm StatusChip plus a plain-text honesty gloss ('3 boards, 1 live' or 'No live board'), so trust density is visible and scannable before any click and is never color-only. No card grid (it re-implies a ranked league table and is new chrome), no model name and no score anywhere on the explorer (which enforces honest-picks-only at the architecture level: the first time any model is named is inside a use-case page, welded to its date and caveat). Honest-empty use cases stay in the list at full weight, grouped last under a quiet HairRule and an 11px label ('Tasks no benchmark measures cleanly yet'), so thinness reads as a deliberate authored section, not 6 holes. Placement: layer this in as a 'Pick by task' block above the untouched Standings on the same /benchmarks page (or a co-equal door), NEVER as a default that demotes the trust list and NEVER by relocating The Standings to a new route.
critique.bestPickShape: A HonestPick row that is the EXPORTED PR1 ScoreStamp atom (currently module-local: export it plus FlagChip, monYear, domainOf with no signature change) wrapped with one claim label on top and one mandatory caveat line beneath, hairline-separated (borderBottom var(--color-border-subtle)), NO box, NO medal, NO vs-#N delta. Top to bottom: (A) a claim label at 15px 600 ('Best for minimizing made-up facts') preceded by the backing board's sm StatusChip, so STATUS is the hero of the pick exactly as it is of every PR1 row; (B-D) the verbatim ScoreStamp three lines (model + lab / verbatim score string + baseline gloss / monYear(asOfDate) + domainOf(sourceUrl) link) with inline SELF-REPORTED (steel) and DISPUTED (gold) FlagChips, and a new SCAFFOLD chip via the generic FlagChip(label, tile) using an existing tile pair when the tested unit is an agent scaffold not a bare model; (E) one always-present caveat line, gold-hued when it is a real warning, stating the single caveat the pick rests on, plus a 'what was tested' clause for scaffolds. The pick's VERB is a deterministic function of the backing board's trust tier (a PICK_VERB lookup defined once beside TRUST_META): live tops; near_ceiling leads-but-the-top-is-a-tie; contested/self-reported claims-the-top-spot plus conflict caveat; dated led-as-of-MON-YYYY in past tense; near-tie demotes a single name to a co-leaders framing reusing the PR1 near-tie line. A pick MAY claim only 'top of THIS one named board, on THIS date, by THIS metric', as loud as the tier allows; it MAY NOT claim best-model-in-general, any cross-benchmark or composite standing, any cost/speed/context superlative, any rank inside a near-tie, or a scaffold score as a bare-model score. Crucially, the receipt (score, date, source, flags) is RESOLVED from the cited benchmark's leaders Json at render time, not stored separately, so a pick can never drift from its source board; honest-empty boards emit ZERO picks and the slot renders the reused HonestEmpty body with the dated anchor instead. Strip the bold name and you are left with a true, dated, sourced sentence.

synthesis.designLanguageName: By Task, with Receipts (the Contents Page spine: a hairline use-case index layered over The Standings, and a decision-walk use-case page where every honest pick is a welded ScoreStamp citing one benchmark, one date, one caveat)
synthesis.rationale:
The spine is editorial-minimalist's "The Contents Page": a single-column hairline use-case index built by cloning the PR1 BenchmarkRow grammar into a UseCaseRow, and a use-case page that is the PR1 editorial split with an honest-pick rail. I pick it for one reason rooted in the data reality: of 22 benchmarks, 6 ship honest-empty (livecodebench, longbench-v2, global-mmlu, simpleqa, mmmu-pro, design2code per the seed) and 2 more are self-reported-flagged (omnidocbench, arc-agi-2), and the new roster's leaders are mostly dated (BFCL April board), scaffolds (OSWorld), or self-reported (OmniDocBench). So MOST use cases will surface few or no trustworthy live leaders. A card grid advertises that each cell should be full and makes a 1-board-0-live use case read as a broken or unfinished tile next to a 3-live one; a hairline row is NATURALLY allowed to be short, so thinness reads as deliberate teaching, not a hole. All four critics independently killed the benchlm card grid for exactly this reason, and the PR1 panel already rejected a two-up grid for the index one altitude down because a row of equal cells reads as a league table. A column of eleven hairline rows reads as a contents page of doors, never eleven ranked contestants. Three of the four proposals converged on the hairline list; I take it as the spine.

Placement is decided decisively and conservatively. I do NOT repoint /benchmarks to a bare question page and demote The Standings to a new /benchmarks/all route (decision-flow's move): every critic flagged that as the largest churn, it changes the canonical URL of the already-built trust surface, and it relitigates a PR1 placement the brief says not to redesign. Instead, /benchmarks gains one ghost-text toggle pair at the top of the existing header, reusing the exported CategoryTab visual: "By task | The Standings". The PR1 trust list stays at the same URL with its internals byte-for-byte untouched; only its component gains one additive prop and the page fetches both. I default the landing to "By task" because the locked audience (mixed, often non-technical students) arrives with a task, not a benchmark name, but the toggle is one element away and this is the one item I flag to James rather than ship silently (open question 1). This is lighter than reuse-maximalist's Option A, which stacks the explorer above the entire 22-row Standings on one very long page; a toggle keeps both doors at /benchmarks with no scroll tax and no relocation.

The honest-pick mechanism grafts the sharpest idea from each runner-up. From benchlm-data-forward I take the dated bottom-line sentence (the single most decision-ready element in the set) and the SCAFFOLD chip, but I drop the card grid it was packaged with. From decision-flow I take the "What are you trying to do?" interrogative framing for the explorer header, how-to-choose as the SPINE of the page (not a sidebar), and the best idea anywhere: the honest-empty reframe "we are not naming a winner, judge it yourself on these two things" plus two authored criteria, which converts a dead end into a method, which is precisely what a mostly-empty roster needs. From all three I take the trust-tier-gated pick verb (a PICK_VERB lookup mirroring TRUST_META) so claim loudness tracks evidence deterministically.

The one place I deliberately OVERRIDE the reuse-maximalist consensus is pick provenance. Two proposals (benchlm-data-forward, decision-flow) author the pick's score/date as standalone copy that can drift; reuse-maximalist instead RESOLVES the receipt from Benchmark.leaders at render time, which is elegant. But the benchlm-data-forward critic is right that resolve-from-leaders collides with this exact roster: an honest-empty board has no leaders to resolve from, and forcing scaffold or self-reported scores INTO Benchmark.leaders to make them resolvable would change what the PR1 brief renders and could surface a scaffold score AS a leader on the trust-first spine the brief locks. So I take a hybrid: the pick AUTHORS the cited benchmark slug, the leader rank, the claim label, and the required caveat; getUseCaseDetail RESOLVES the score/lab/date/flag chips from that benchmark's actual leaders Json by (slug, rank) at render time, so the receipt can never drift; and when the cited board is honest-empty the pick is authored with empty=true and its own datedAnchor and emits NO model name. This gives drift-proof receipts for the populated minority and authored honesty for the empty majority, without polluting the trust spine.

REPAIRS APPLIED IN THIS PASS. One repo-grounding fix and five trust-integrity fixes, none of which weaken the design; each tightens it.

Repo-grounding fix (1): the model-selection concept was mis-cited as lib/curriculum.ts:734. lib/curriculum.ts does not exist; the slug is seeded at prisma/seed-data/curriculum.ts:734 (line exact, path prefix wrong). Corrected in repoGrounding. Functionally harmless because relatedConcepts validate at render against the live prisma.concept DB table (lib/benchmarks.ts:216), not the seed file, and the eq-bench record already carries {label:'Choosing a model', slug:'model-selection'} at seed line 967, so the GoDeeper link resolves once the concept is seeded.

Trust-integrity fix (1), the most important: the explorer col-3 readout 'most boards live' vs 'no live board' and the answerable/sparse grouping WERE a per-use-case rollup of trust tiers across the 2 to 3 backing boards, a soft cross-benchmark verdict the locked decisions forbid (a reader scans col-3 and reads 'this task scores well'). FIXED: col-3 is no longer derived from any live-board tally. It now prints only a neutral non-comparative affordance ('see the boards ->') and the grouping is gated on the single AUTHORED honestEmpty boolean already on the UseCase model, never on a computed tally over the backing set. The honest-empty group still sits last under the HairRule + label, but membership is an authored editorial fact (UseCase.honestEmpty), not a synthesized count. UseCaseCardData.group is therefore derived from honestEmpty alone, and the evidence-strip StatusChips (each board's OWN status, the per-benchmark hero) carry all trust signal. No phrase ever ranks 'most live' against 'no live'.

Trust-integrity fix (2): the EQ-Bench near-tie 'share the top of' pick risked one PickStamp asserting two models where only one rank was welded, or a 2-row mini-table inside a pick. FIXED: a co-leaders pick (verb 'share the top of') now emits N separate stacked welded ScoreStamps, ONE per cited rank, each its own model + score + date + source, with the single near-tie caveat line beneath the group. PickSeed gains a leaderRanks array (number[]) for the co-leaders case (leaderRank stays the single-leader path); the renderer maps each rank to its own resolved ScoreStamp. 'Share' is thus sourced for every named model and no stamp ever carries an unsourced second name. This is still not a league table: there is no vs-N delta, no medal, no ordering claim beyond 'these two share the top, here is each one's own receipt'.

Trust-integrity fix (3): SCAFFOLD reused the gold tile, colliding chromatically with CONTESTED/Disputed, risking a scaffold score (83.6%) reading as a bare-model score. FIXED without adding a token: the WORD 'SCAFFOLD' carries the distinction (the a11y spine already relies on word-first status), and the mandatory what-was-tested line is promoted to a REQUIRED, seeder-enforced field. seed-use-cases.ts now rejects any pick with scaffold=true and an empty whatWasTested exactly as it rejects an empty caveat, and the renderer prints whatWasTested as a hard gold caveat line E ('led by a scaffold, Pointer Agent running Opus 4.7; you cannot get this score from the bare model'). Color reuse is acceptable only because the mandatory word disambiguates, and that mandate is now locked in the data layer.

Trust-integrity fix (4): getUseCaseDetail's silent drop-to-empty on a failed (slug,rank) resolve could mask a broken citation as authored honest-empty. FIXED: authored-empty (empty=true in the seed, intentional) and resolve-failure-empty are now distinct paths. Only a seed-authored empty=true pick renders the judge-it-yourself / HonestEmpty body. A non-empty pick whose (slug,rank) fails to resolve (board unpublished or leaders[rank] gone) DROPS entirely (never rendered as honest-empty content), logs an admin-visible warning, and FAILS seed-use-cases --verify (which already requires every leaderRank to resolve). Absence is never silently presented as authored honesty.

Trust-integrity fix (5): the rail's 'newest pick date across the use case' stamp plus a single-leader Bottom Line read as a use-case-wide synthesized 'current best'. FIXED: the populated Bottom Line is bound to exactly ONE named benchmark and that board's own date (the primary board the sentence names), authored in the seed (bottomLineBenchmarkSlug), never a max-across-picks reduction. The rail no longer computes a page-level 'As of MON YYYY' by reducing over picks; if the rail shows a date it is the date OF the specific board named in the Bottom Line, labeled with that board's name, so it cannot read as a use-case-wide 'as of'. No min/max over multiple boards appears anywhere.

Net: STATUS stays the hero (of the explorer row via each board's own StatusChip, never a synthesized verdict; and of each pick via a leading StatusChip); the welded ScoreStamp stays the hero of every receipt and a co-leaders pick emits one welded stamp per name; honest-empty is full-weight authored content (authored, not tallied) with a judge-it-yourself method; and the whole thing reuses PR1 primitives with zero new card system, zero new color token, and one tiny justified lookup. No composite, no catalog, no cross-use-case tally, no soft cross-benchmark verdict ever appears.

referencePicks.name: Wirecutter (NYT) best-for guidance and honest negative space
referencePicks.why: Borrow the receipted best-for structure (a named pick tied to one stated basis and a tested reason) and especially the willingness to publish we-do-not-recommend-anything-here-yet. This is the gold standard for our honest-empty pick block and the receipted PickStamp. Adapt the voice to editorial trust-first; ignore the affiliate-commerce framing and the star-rating composite.

referencePicks.name: Our World in Data, indicator page pattern
referencePicks.why: Best-in-class at welding source-and-date to every number and at making gaps legible (explicit no-data labels with a prose reason), which maps one-to-one onto the welded ScoreStamp receipt and the honest-empty use-case body. It is also the PR1 synthesis reference, so the use-case surface stays in the same lineage.

referencePicks.name: benchlm.ai use-case explorer and per-use-case pages
referencePicks.why: Borrow the information design: a use-case entry point, backing benchmarks under each facet, the dated bottom-line sentence, decision-ready picks, and the per-goal how-to-choose block. ADAPT to trust-first by attaching a receipt and a trust-gated verb to every pick. REJECT its 4-column card wall (use the hairline index), its single composite score, and its Full Rankings list with score bars and vs-N deltas: those are the locked-out league-table moves.

referencePicks.name: FiveThirtyEight forecasting and too-close-to-call presentation
referencePicks.why: Borrow the discipline of expressing uncertainty as a first-class output and never ordering what the data cannot order. Directly informs the near-tie demotion (EQ-Bench #1/#2 within 12.5 Elo, BFCL #2/#3 within 0.73 pt, OSWorld top-3 within ~2 pt) and the trust-gated pick verbs. The numbers-with-humility register is the right voice for caveat-forward picks.

referencePicks.name: MLPerf / MLCommons benchmark reporting rules
referencePicks.why: Borrow the requirement to disclose the exact configuration and harness behind any number and the open/closed split that separates tuned systems from bare components. Directly informs the SCAFFOLD chip and the what-was-tested line for OSWorld (Pointer Agent on Opus 4.7, not the bare model). Avoid its engineering density: we want one plain caveat line, not a results manifest.

referencePicks.name: GOV.UK Design System, task-based start pages and plain-language patterns
referencePicks.why: Model for task-framed entry points and short declarative caveat sentences written for a mixed, non-expert audience. Supports framing each facet by the student task (not making things up) rather than the benchmark name, and writing the dated/self-reported/scaffold caveats as plain sentences, not hedged marketing copy.

explorerSurface.concept:
A single-column hairline index of the 11 use cases at --maxw-content (1040px), data-surface="editorial" inside the (main) data-theme="light" shell, built by cloning the PR1 BenchmarkRow grammar into a new UseCaseRow. NOT a benchlm card grid: eleven equal cards read as ranked comparable peers (the league-table read the PR1 panel already rejected for the benchmark index), and on this mostly-empty roster a sparse card looks broken while a sparse hairline row reads as deliberate. The explorer is one of two doors at /benchmarks via a single ghost-text toggle (the exported CategoryTab visual): [By task] | The Standings. Default landing is By task because the audience arrives with a task; The Standings is the unchanged PR1 trust list one click away.

HEADER (decision-flow interrogative framing, reusing the BenchmarksClient header stack): SectionEyebrow "Pick a model" + h1 "What are you trying to do?" at clamp(34px,4vw,44px) 600 -0.02em + a 680px lead p in --color-text-2: "Start from your task. Each one names the two or three tests that actually measure it, says how far you can trust each, and gives one honest pick with its date and its catch, or says plainly when no one can." A top-right editorial-link "Browse all 22 benchmarks ->" (the .editorial-link sweep + ArrowRight) routes to the The Standings toggle, mirroring benchlm's Browse-all without its confident tone.

USECASEROW (grid 36px / minmax(0,1fr) / 172px, columnGap 20, full-row absolute Link to /benchmarks/use/[slug], identical to BenchmarkRow so it inherits the <=600px collapse and benchCellIn capped-18ms stagger for free):
- col 1 (36px): the tile-indigo badge as a POSITION dot or single-letter task glyph, never an ordinal that reads as a rank (ordering is editorial, never numbered as quality).
- col 2 (1fr): use-case name as the .editorial-link .bench-name hero at --text-xl, plain student-task language ("Factual accuracy: not making things up"); a one-line audienceLine in 14px --color-text-2 ellipsised ("Will it answer a fact wrong with confidence?"); then the EVIDENCE STRIP replacing PR1's DOMAIN . scoreType line: the 2 to 3 backing benchmark short names, each followed by its size="sm" StatusChip in TRUST_TAB_ORDER, so before any click the reader sees Coding carries three boards each with its OWN status chip while Long context carries one DATED chip. Each chip is that single board's own per-benchmark STATUS (the PR1 hero), never a verdict synthesized across the backing set. NO model name and NO score ever appears on this surface, which enforces honest-picks-only at the architecture level.
- col 3 (172px, the bench-status slot): a NEUTRAL non-comparative affordance only, never a derived verdict and never a live-board tally. It reads exactly "See the boards ->" in --color-text-2 for every row (the trust signal lives entirely in the per-board evidence-strip chips, where STATUS is already the hero). REPAIR: the earlier 'most boards live' / 'no live board' wording was a soft cross-benchmark rollup of trust tiers and is removed; col-3 carries no count-derived language at all.

GROUPING (REPAIRED): the rows are split into two groups by the single AUTHORED UseCase.honestEmpty boolean, NOT by any computed live-board count over the backing set. Use cases with honestEmpty=true sit last under a HairRule + an 11px --color-text-3 label "Tasks no benchmark measures cleanly yet". Membership is an editorial fact the author sets in the seed, so the explorer never ranks tasks by a tally; within each group, order is UseCase.order then name.

No trust-filter tab row here (the PR1 CategoryTab filters by trust tier, meaningless on a use-case axis). No dynamic filter UI: 11 authored items is a knowable set, a faceted-search panel would imply an open queryable dataset and clash with the editorial voice.

explorerSurface.livesWhere: REWORK of placement only, not of the spine. /benchmarks stays one route. A thin new BenchmarksHubClient owns the two-mode toggle and renders either UseCaseExplorerClient (By task) or the UNCHANGED BenchmarksClient (The Standings). The page.tsx server route now fetches both getBenchmarks and getUseCases and passes both into the hub. The only edit to benchmarks-client.tsx is exporting CategoryTab (lift from module-local, no signature change) plus exporting a PICK_VERB map beside TRUST_META; its trust spine, BenchmarkRow, sort (TRUST_ORDER), honest-empty state, and trust-filter tabs are byte-for-byte unchanged. The sidebar keeps the single /benchmarks item: isActive is a prefix match (pathname===href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/")), so /benchmarks/use/* keeps it active with no new nav. This honors do-not-redesign-the-trust-spine while answering the brief's openness to reworking the surface: the trust list becomes one of two doors, never replaced or relocated.

useCasePage.concept: /benchmarks/use/[slug]: a DECISION WALK rendered as the PR1 two-column editorial split (.trend-detail-split + .trend-detail-rule, collapsing to one column and hiding the rule at <=720px), .trend-detail-enter fade, data-surface="editorial", --maxw-content, padding 40px 40px 96px. It mirrors benchmark-detail-client.tsx beat-for-beat so it inherits the voice for free. The left column is the prose spine (how-to-choose is the spine, not a sidebar); the right rail is the honest-picks panel (the analog of LeaderPanel). The route clones benchmarks/[slug]/page.tsx exactly: dynamic=force-dynamic, metadata title 'Benchmarks | AISA Atlas', await params (Promise), getBenchmarkViewer then getUseCaseDetail(slug, viewer), if (!useCase) redirect('/benchmarks').
sections: Kicker eyebrow "USE CASE" + DraftChip when the use-case row is draft and viewer isAdmin
h1 = use-case name (clamp 30-40px) + one bold 19px/500 task lead (the audienceLine expanded)

BOTTOM LINE (benchlm's signature, made honest AND single-board-bound). REPAIR: bound to exactly ONE authored benchmark (UseCase.bottomLineBenchmarkSlug) and that board's own date, never a max-across-picks reduction. SectionEyebrow "Bottom line" + a left-border callout (3px, NOT a tinted box) whose sentence is trust-gated and ALWAYS dated to that one named board. Populated: "As of JUN 2026, claude-fable-5 leads EQ-Bench Creative Writing, the one independent writing board we trust, but its top two are within 12.5 Elo, so read it as a cluster." The date in this sentence is eq-bench-creative-writing's own asOfDate, not a use-case-wide stamp. Honest-empty: "No board here measures this cleanly, so we are not naming a leader. Here is the yardstick and the most recent anchor."

BEAT 1 "The tests that matter": one Prose sentence naming the ordered backing benchmarks BEFORE any leader appears (goal-to-evidence routing), then a YARDSTICK LIST (hairline rows, NOT full BenchmarkRows): each = a size=sm StatusChip (that board's OWN status) + the benchmark name as an .editorial-link to /benchmarks/[slug] + a one-line how-far-to-trust gloss, with an inline "also central to Science" cross-tag note where shared. Order is trust-then-authored, never score, and never a synthesized cross-board verdict.

BEAT 2 "How to choose" (THE SPINE, full weight): a per-goal branch list, each branch a sub-goal -> exactly one benchmark or an honest "no clean answer yet". Splits only on task shape a benchmark measures. Metric disagreement becomes TWO branches (EQ-Bench Elo vs Rubric).

BEAT 3 "What to watch" (the one structural recolor): the WatchOut primitive (gold eyebrow + 3px gold bar) carrying the use-case-level caveat ("Two of these boards are saturated; a high score no longer separates the top models", or "The only board here is dated to April 2026").

GoDeeper: concept cross-links validated against the live catalog (reuses relatedConcepts validation against prisma.concept; model-selection / benchmarking-llms / filtering-ai-hype all exist in the seeded curriculum).

RIGHT RAIL: a board-bound dated stamp showing the BOTTOM-LINE board's name + its own "As of MON YYYY" (never a max-across-picks reduction, never "top model is X") on a hairline, then the hairline-separated PickStamp column (never a podium, never a vs-N delta), then a PanelFooter-style line listing the backing StatusChips + "These leaders move; dates are when each board was last readable." A co-leaders pick emits one welded ScoreStamp per cited rank. Honest-empty rail = the PR1 HonestEmpty body verbatim + the dated anchor + the judge-it-yourself criteria.
Admin: a UseCaseModeration card (copy of BenchmarkModeration) at the foot, PATCH /api/admin/use-cases.

honestPicks.concept: A pick is a claim welded to one receipt, rendered as a PickStamp: a thin wrapper around the PR1 ScoreStamp atom (exported from benchmark-detail-client.tsx with FlagChip, monYear, domainOf, plus the prose primitives) with a claim label above and a required caveat line below, in a hairline-separated row (borderBottom var(--color-border-subtle), NO box, NO medal, NO vs-N delta). STATUS is the hero of the pick exactly as it is of every PR1 row. A SINGLE-leader pick emits ONE welded ScoreStamp; a CO-LEADERS pick (near-tie) emits ONE welded ScoreStamp PER cited rank so every named model carries its own full receipt.
honestPicks.whatItMayClaim: ONLY: top of THIS one named board, on THIS date, by THIS metric, subject to THIS caveat. The leading verb is a deterministic function of the cited benchmark's trust tier, authored once in a PICK_VERB lookup that mirrors TRUST_META: live -> "tops"; near_ceiling -> "leads, but the top is a statistical tie"; contested or selfReported -> "claims the top of" + the conflict caveat; dated -> "led as of MON YYYY" in past tense; a near-tie at the top demotes any single name to a co-leaders framing ("share the top of") AND emits a separate welded ScoreStamp for each co-leader cited, so 'share' is sourced for every name. The score, lab, date, and flag chips RESOLVE at render from the cited benchmark's leaders Json by (slug, rank), so the receipt can never drift from the board.
honestPicks.honestEmptyAndSelfReported: HONEST-EMPTY board (AUTHORED empty=true only): the pick is authored with empty=true and emits NO model name; the slot renders the PR1 HonestEmpty body + the single dated anchor + the judge-it-yourself criteria, at full slot weight (LongBench v2: human experts 53.7%, best direct model 50.1%, chance 25%). REPAIR: only a seed-authored empty=true pick may render this body. A non-empty pick whose (slug,rank) fails to resolve (board unpublished or leaders[rank] gone) is NOT shown as honest-empty; it drops entirely, logs an admin-visible warning, and fails seed-use-cases --verify. SELF-REPORTED-with-conflict (OmniDocBench, whose #1 MinerU2.5-Pro shares maintainer OpenDataLab with the benchmark): a pick IS emitted but the verb is "claims the top of", it carries the Self-reported (steel) FlagChip, and line E states the conflict verbatim. DATED board (BFCL, April board): verb "led as of APR 2026", line E "board last readable April 2026, predates Opus 4.7/4.8, Gemini 3.1, GPT-5.5". SCAFFOLD (OSWorld #1 Pointer Agent w/ Opus 4.7): the SCAFFOLD FlagChip via the generic FlagChip(label, tile='gold') PLUS a MANDATORY, seeder-enforced what-was-tested line ("led by a scaffold, Pointer Agent running Opus 4.7; you cannot get this score from the bare model") rendered as a hard gold caveat line E. REPAIR: SCAFFOLD reuses the gold tile (which CONTESTED/Disputed also use), so chromatic distinction is NOT relied upon; the WORD 'SCAFFOLD' carries the meaning and the seeder rejects any scaffold=true pick whose whatWasTested is empty, exactly as it rejects an empty caveat, so a scaffold score can never read as a bare-model score. The four states stay verbally distinct (the words tops / claims / led-as-of / scaffold) even where two share a hue.
honestPicks.whatItMayNotClaim: NEVER: best model in general; any composite, average, or cross-benchmark standing; any tops-N-of-11 tally; any per-use-case live-board count or 'most boards live' verdict (col-3 carries none); any rank order inside a near-tie (the per-rank welded stamps plus the near-tie note replace the order); any price/speed/latency/context superlative (no model catalog); a scaffold score as a bare-model score; a single PickStamp asserting a second model that does not carry its own welded receipt. The caveat is a REQUIRED field the seeder rejects when empty (and whatWasTested is REQUIRED when scaffold=true), and it is part of the welded block, so the flattering half (the model name) is physically un-screenshot-able without the qualifying half.
honestPicks.receipts: line A (claim, trust-gated): size=sm StatusChip of the cited board (that board's own status) + "Best for {sub-goal}" MicroLabel. line B (resolved): model + lab prefixed by the PICK_VERB verb, with inline SELF-REPORTED / DISPUTED / SCAFFOLD FlagChips; for a co-leaders pick, line B repeats once per cited rank, each as its own welded ScoreStamp. lines C-D (resolved ScoreStamp): verbatim score string + baselineGloss, then monYear(asOfDate) + domainOf(sourceUrl) link. line E (caveat, required, gold-hued when a hard warning): the single caveat the pick rests on + the mandatory what-was-tested clause for scaffolds.
honestPicks.cardShape: hairline row, no box / no medal / no delta; claim label + StatusChip on top, the welded three ScoreStamp lines in the middle (resolved from the cited benchmark's leaders; one welded stamp per cited rank for a co-leaders pick), one required gold caveat line at the bottom.

dataModel.prismaChanges:
Two changes to prisma/schema.prisma (after db push + generate, kill and restart next dev on 3100; the .prisma/client HMR cannot hot-reload, symptom is a 500 'Cannot read properties of undefined'). (1) The useCases tag: add `useCases Json?  // string[] of use-case slugs this benchmark backs` to model Benchmark (~line 551, beside relatedConcepts/sources). Json, not String[] and not a join table, to match the existing inline-Json convention (leaders, relatedConcepts, sources are all inline Json) and keep the delta to one nullable column; parsed via the existing asStringArray helper. (2) A second model mirroring Benchmark's draft-gate + inline-Json shape so it inherits the publish path and dash-validation for free:

model UseCase {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  slug          String   @unique          // /benchmarks/use/[slug]
  name          String                     // "Factual accuracy: not making things up"
  audienceLine  String                     // one-line student gloss (explorer + page lead)
  taskLead      String                     // the bold bus-stop framing sentence
  bottomLine    String                     // the dated bottom-line callout (authored, dated, trust-gated copy)
  bottomLineBenchmarkSlug String?           // REPAIR: the ONE board the bottom line + rail date bind to (null when honestEmpty)
  evidenceLine  String                     // "read mainly through SimpleQA, with HLE as a cross-check"
  yardsticks    Json                       // [{ benchmarkSlug, trustGloss }] ordered (the tests that matter)
  picks         Json                       // [PickSeed] authored honest picks
  howToChoose   Json                       // [{ goal, benchmarkSlug?, guidance, noCleanAnswer?, reason? }]
  judgeCriteria Json?                       // string[]; the honest-empty "judge it yourself on these two things"
  watchOut      String
  watchOutUrl   String?
  relatedConcepts Json?                     // [{ label, slug? }] reuse the validated shape
  honestEmpty   Boolean  @default(false)    // AUTHORED: no backing board has a usable live leader; drives explorer grouping
  datedAnchor   String?                     // the single anchor shown when honestEmpty
  order         Int      @default(0)        // editorial sort within a group
  status        String   @default("draft")  // publish gate, mirrors Benchmark
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
  @@index([status])
  @@map("use_cases")
}

PickSeed (one element of picks[]): { label, benchmarkSlug, leaderRank? (cite one leader row for a single-leader pick), leaderRanks? (number[]; REPAIR: cite multiple rows for a co-leaders near-tie pick so each gets its own welded ScoreStamp), scaffold? (bool), selfReported? (bool; for the verb), empty? (bool; AUTHORED honest-empty), datedAnchor? (when empty), caveat (REQUIRED), whatWasTested? (REQUIRED when scaffold=true) }. The pick AUTHORS the slug+rank(s)+label+caveat; the score/lab/date/flags RESOLVE from the cited benchmark's leaders at render, so a pick can never drift and a scaffold/self-reported score is never forced onto Benchmark.leaders. A co-leaders pick uses leaderRanks and the renderer emits one welded ScoreStamp per rank.

graftsFromRunnersUp: From benchlm-data-forward-honest: the dated BOTTOM LINE sentence at the top of every use-case page (the single most decision-ready element in the set), reimplemented as a left-border callout (not a tinted box), trust-gated and always dated, BOUND TO ONE NAMED BENCHMARK (bottomLineBenchmarkSlug) and its own date (the trust-integrity repair), swapping to 'no board measures this cleanly' for empties. KILLED its packaging: the repeat(2) card-grid explorer and the .usecase-grid class (new chrome on a cardless surface; sparse cards read as broken).

From benchlm-data-forward-honest: the SCAFFOLD FlagChip + a MANDATORY (seeder-enforced) what-was-tested line for OSWorld, implemented via the existing generic FlagChip(label, tile='gold') so it adds a third word to the vocabulary with no new token; the WORD carries the meaning so the shared gold hue with Contested/Disputed never collapses it into a dismissible read.

From decision-flow-first: the interrogative explorer header 'What are you trying to do?' so a mixed-background student grabs a verb before meeting any trust vocabulary; how-to-choose as the page SPINE (not a sidebar); and the best idea anywhere, the honest-empty reframe 'we are not naming a winner, judge it yourself on these two things' + two authored criteria, which converts a dead end into a method (essential on a mostly-empty roster). KILLED its repoint of /benchmarks to a bare question page and the relocation of The Standings to /benchmarks/all (largest churn; relitigates PR1 placement).

From decision-flow-first: a pure-export PR with a Playwright screenshot-diff baseline on the unchanged /benchmarks/[slug] brief, to de-risk exporting ScoreStamp/prose primitives without destabilizing PR1 (folded into PR2a acceptance).

From reuse-maximalist: resolve the pick's receipt (score/lab/date/flags) from the cited benchmark's leaders Json at render time so it can never drift, but HYBRIDIZED with authored slug+rank(s)+caveat+empty-state so honest-empty boards (which have no leaders to resolve) and scaffold/self-reported scores never get forced onto the PR1 trust spine. A co-leaders pick resolves and welds one ScoreStamp PER cited rank (the trust-integrity repair) so 'share the top' is sourced for every named model. A non-empty pick that fails to resolve drops with an admin warning rather than masquerading as authored honest-empty (the trust-integrity repair). This answers the benchlm critic's objection to a naive resolve-from-leaders.

From reuse-maximalist: store Benchmark.useCases as Json? (matching the inline-Json convention of leaders/relatedConcepts/sources) rather than a join table or String[], keeping the schema delta to one nullable column parsed by the existing asStringArray helper. KILLED its Option A (explorer stacked above the entire 22-row Standings, a very long page) and its rendering of full BenchmarkRows as the use-case yardsticks (couples the surfaces; use a lighter StatusChip+name+gloss line).

From all three runners-up plus the PR1 lineage: a single UseCase Prisma model mirroring Benchmark's draft-gate + admin publish route (not a static map, not a Json blob on another model), so the content layer inherits the publish gate, @@index([status]) visibility, DraftChip, and BANNED_DASHES seed validation for free; getUseCaseDetail validates every cited benchmarkSlug against live published benchmarks and relatedConcepts against the live prisma.concept table exactly like the PR1 detail path; and the PICK_VERB lookup is defined once beside TRUST_META (no third copy of the trust order).

resolvedQuestions.question: Default landing mode at /benchmarks
resolvedQuestions.answer: Keep By task as the default (James, 2026-06-24). The Standings is the one-click second tab.

resolvedQuestions.question: 11 vs 12 use cases and the optional Studying and tutoring case
resolvedQuestions.answer: Scrap Studying and tutoring (James): no benchmark is a clear enough measure of teaching quality to anchor it. Ship the 11. PR2d drops the extra case and becomes an optional copy pass only.

resolvedQuestions.question: Honest-empty presentation under a sparse roster
resolvedQuestions.answer: Present what IS known first (the dated anchor and any baseline figures, so the student has something to work with), THEN state the gap and WHY it exists (an authored gapReason field on UseCase), THEN the judge-it-yourself criteria (James). Ship all 11; do not stage the launch.

resolvedQuestions.question: Pick freshness vs the future leader cron
resolvedQuestions.answer: James deferred; decided default, revisit when the cron is wired: v1 is static-authored so nothing drifts in v1; the verb is computed from trust (PICK_VERB) so it self-updates, the authored caveat can lag. When the future cron drafts a changed benchmark, the admin re-reads that benchmark's dependent use-case picks in the same review; a future enhancement can list the picks whose cited board changed. Not v1-blocking.

resolvedQuestions.question: Cross-tagging display
resolvedQuestions.answer: James's call to the panel: keep only the inline 'also central to X' navigational note, no cross-use-case rollup or count (a backdoor composite, killed on sight).

resolvedQuestions.question: Per-use-case ordered benchmark list and trust gloss wording
resolvedQuestions.answer: James's call: author the taxonomy mapping as proposed; James reviews the ordering and the gloss wording on-site after the build and requests changes from there.

resolvedQuestions.question: Co-leaders pick depth on a tie
resolvedQuestions.answer: If the top is a tie, make it known no matter how many models tie (James): weld every tied rank as its own welded ScoreStamp, no cap. leaderRanks lists all tied ranks and the single near-tie caveat spans the group.

openQuestions: Default landing mode at /benchmarks. I default the toggle to 'By task' because the audience arrives with a task, which makes The Standings the second (one-click) tab. This is the one product decision I flag rather than ship silently: confirm By-task-default, OR default to The Standings (returning users keep their muscle memory), OR show both as co-equal doors with no default. Reversible in one line in BenchmarksHubClient.

The 11-vs-12 use-case set and the optional 'Studying and tutoring' honest-empty case. The taxonomy gives 11; Studying-and-tutoring is a deliberate honest-empty lesson (no benchmark cleanly measures teaching quality) that strengthens the thesis but adds a 12th thin row. Include it in v1 (PR2d) or hold it? Also confirm the exact 11 slug names and the plain-language task labels (e.g. 'Factual accuracy: not making things up' vs 'Will it make things up').

Authoring volume and the honest-empty proportion. Each use case needs an authored audienceLine, taskLead, dated bottomLine (bound to one named board), evidenceLine, a yardstick gloss per backing benchmark, 1 to 3 how-to-choose branches, and either 1 to 2 receipted picks or (for ~5 of 11) the honest-empty body + judge-it-yourself criteria. Confirm this is acceptable as a deliberate stance and that the cumulative thin-page effect reads as teaching, not an unfinished feature, OR stage the launch to the best-evidenced use cases first (coding, writing, agents, science).

Pick freshness vs the PR1 cron. Picks resolve from Benchmark.leaders today, so when the PR2 leader cron later refreshes a board, the use-case pick's score/date update automatically but the authored caveat/verb do NOT (a board could flip from dated to live, or a near-tie could resolve, and the prose would lag). Confirm whether a use case should set its own curatedAt-style lock or whether an admin re-reads picks after each cron run (mirrors the same open question PR1 flagged for benchmark leaders).

Cross-tagging display. A benchmark in multiple use cases (humanitys-last-exam in Science + Factuality; lmarena-chatbot-arena in Preference + Writing) shows as honest shared membership with an inline 'also central to X' note. Confirm there is no appetite for any cross-use-case rollup (a 'this board backs N use cases' count is a backdoor composite and is killed on sight); the note is purely a navigational cross-link via the existing editorial-link sweep.

Which benchmarks back each use case, and the trust gloss wording per pairing. The tag set (which of the 22 each use case draws on, and in what order) drives the evidence strip and the yardstick list. I propose the taxonomy's mapping (e.g. Coding -> swe-bench-verified, livecodebench, humaneval) but James should ratify the per-use-case ordered benchmark list and approve the one-line how-far-to-trust gloss for each, since that wording is the literacy payload and must stay dash-free.

Co-leaders pick presentation depth. The near-tie repair emits one welded ScoreStamp per cited rank (e.g. EQ-Bench top two). Confirm two stamps is the right ceiling; if a board ever has a 3-way tie at the top, do we weld all three (taller pick) or cap at two and let the caveat carry the third? I default to welding exactly the ranks the author cites in leaderRanks, capped by editorial judgement, never an open-ended podium.

repoGrounding.summary:
Repo-grounding verification of the BENCHMARKS_USE_CASES v2 synthesis against the live aisa-atlas repo: 33 of 34 grounding claims verified OK, 1 wrong (a file-path citation, not a design flaw).

WHAT HELD (the load-bearing spine is solid):
- Every verified token is exact at the cited line: --maxw-content 1040px (88), --color-bg #FAF9F5 (99), --color-border #E8E5DC (103) / -subtle #EFEDE6 (104), --color-text-2/-3 both #6B6B73 (112-113), --color-accent #4255FF under [data-surface=editorial] (191) hover #3545E0 (192), --color-gold #B8860C (123) / -soft #FBEFCE (124), and all five StatusChip tile pairs (sage 134-135, steel 160-161, gold 140-141, stone 158-159, indigo 154-155). --tile-slate is confirmed ABSENT.
- The layout-class decision is correct: .trend-detail-split + .trend-detail-rule collapse to 1fr and hide the rule at <=720px (460-470) so it IS right for the page; .concept-shell hides its > aside at <=1279px (279-285) so it IS fatal for a rail and correctly rejected; .browse-grid is flex/column (336-339), confirming no editorial card grid exists and the hairline-row argument stands.
- All 23 reused primitives (TRUST_META, StatusChip, ScoreStamp, FlagChip, monYear, domainOf, Beat/Prose/WatchOut/GoDeeper/HonestEmpty/LeaderPanel/BenchmarkModeration, CategoryTab, etc.) exist at the EXACT cited line numbers; FlagChip is genuinely generic over tile so SCAFFOLD via tile=gold needs no new token.
- The data/route/admin patterns are all real and copyable: Benchmark model 525-563 (useCases genuinely new), force-dynamic + 'Benchmarks | AISA Atlas' title + Promise params + redirect-on-miss route shape, the non-redirecting requireAdmin->403 PATCH gate with a 501 POST stub, and the seed pipeline's BANNED_DASHES + DOMAINS/SCORE_TYPES/TRUSTS validators (v2 domain additions already present). useCases, UseCase model, lib/use-cases.ts, the use/[slug] route, the admin/use-cases route, and the seeders are all correctly NEW.
- The honest-pick design is backed by real seed data: eq-bench-creative-writing (trust live, nearTie true) has leaders fable-5 Elo 2191.8 / opus-4-7 Elo 2179.3 (12.5 behind) / gpt-5.5 Elo 2019.0, asOfDate 2026-06-23 -> JUN 2026, exactly matching the PickStamp ASCII, and the leaders Json shape supports (slug, rank) render-time resolution. 22 records, 6 honestEmpty, all 12 new-roster slugs present.

THE ONE DEFECT (cosmetic, fix the citation): the synthesis says the model-selection concept lives at 'curriculum.ts:734' implying lib/curriculum.ts. lib/curriculum.ts does not exist; the slug is at prisma/seed-data/curriculum.ts:734 (line number exact, path prefix wrong). This does not break the EQ-Bench 'Choosing a model' GoDeeper link, because relatedConcepts are validated at render against the live prisma.concept DB table (lib/benchmarks.ts:216), not the seed file, and the eq-bench record already carries the {label:'Choosing a model', slug:'model-selection'} pair (seed line 967). Just correct the path in repoGrounding.

No hallucinated tokens, no missing primitives, no wrong reuse target, no dash violations. Plan is cleared to build with that single path-citation fix.

violations.location: explorerSurface.concept col-3 readout + asciiSketch: 'most boards live' vs 'no live board'; and groupBy('answerable'|'sparse') in dataModel getUseCases
violations.issue: The word-first readout 'most boards live' is a count-derived verdict computed across the 2 to 3 backing benchmarks of a use case (a per-use-case aggregation of trust tiers). 'most boards live' is literally 'majority of this use case's boards are LIVE', which is a normalization/rollup across benchmarks rendered as a single judgement on the row. The synthesis itself flags the risk ('never a derived numeric verdict... GROUPED, not sorted by a live-board count') but then still prints a phrase that IS a derived qualitative verdict from the same count, and the data layer computes group:'answerable'|'sparse' from the backing set. A reader scans col-3 and reads it as 'this task scores well', i.e. a soft cross-benchmark league signal.
violations.lockedDecisionViolated: No averaging or normalization across benchmarks; no cross-benchmark composite; STATUS (per-benchmark) is the hero, not a synthesized verdict.
violations.fix: Make col-3 a non-comparative state label drawn from the single weakest/most-relevant board's own STATUS, or drop the phrase entirely and let the evidence-strip chips speak. If a grouping is needed, gate it on a binary authored honestEmpty flag per use case (already in the UseCase model), NOT on a live-board tally over the backing set. Never print a word that ranks 'most live' against 'no live'; print only each board's own StatusChip plus a neutral 'see the boards' affordance.

violations.location: honestPicks.whatItMayClaim + asciiSketch 'share the top of EQ-Bench' / 'co-leaders framing'
violations.issue: For the EQ-Bench near-tie the verb resolves to 'share the top of'. But the resolved receipt for that single PickStamp renders ONE model (claude-fable-5, rank 1) with its Elo, while the prose says 'share the top'. If only rank-1 is welded into the stamp, 'share' is unsourced for the second name; if both rank-1 and rank-2 are welded into one stamp, the stamp stops being the single-benchmark single-row ScoreStamp and becomes a 2-row mini-table inside one pick (the thin end of a league table). Either way one PickStamp now implies an ordering/equivalence claim over two leaders without each carrying its own full welded receipt.
violations.lockedDecisionViolated: Every honest pick must cite a SINGLE benchmark + date + caveat via the welded ScoreStamp; no invited false comparison / mini-ranking.
violations.fix: Resolve and weld BOTH co-leaders as two separate stacked ScoreStamps inside the one 'share the top' pick (each its own model+score+date+source), so 'share' is sourced for both names, and keep the near-tie caveat line. Do not render a single stamp whose prose claims two models. The synthesis must state explicitly that a co-leaders pick emits N welded ScoreStamps (one per cited rank), never one stamp plus an unsourced second name.

violations.issue: SCAFFOLD reuses the gold tile, which is the SAME tile as CONTESTED status and the Disputed FlagChip. On the OSWorld pick a student sees a gold SCAFFOLD chip next to a board whose StatusChip is LIVE (sage); two different gold meanings (disputed vs scaffold) collapse chromatically, and the synthesis's own promise that 'the four states stay verbally and chromatically distinct so they never collapse into one dismissible grey' is broken for scaffold-vs-disputed. More importantly, the resolved score (83.6%) is a SCAFFOLD score; if the chip's color reads as merely 'contested' a reader may treat the scaffold number as a normal model score, exactly the locked-out 'scaffold score as a bare-model score'.
violations.lockedDecisionViolated: Honest-picks-only: a scaffold score may never read as a bare-model score; self-reported/scaffold reality must stay legible, not collapse into one hue.
violations.fix: Keep SCAFFOLD verbally distinct (the word 'SCAFFOLD' carries it, which the a11y note already relies on) but ensure the mandatory what-was-tested line ('Pointer Agent running Opus 4.7; you cannot get this score from the bare model') is REQUIRED and rendered as a hard gold caveat line E, not optional. Confirm the seeder rejects an OSWorld/scaffold pick whose whatWasTested is empty, the same way it rejects an empty caveat. Color reuse is acceptable only because the word disambiguates; lock that the word is mandatory.

violations.location: dataModel.useCaseContentLayer getUseCaseDetail: 'a deleted/unpublished cited board drops the pick to an empty state rather than dead-citing'
violations.issue: Silent fallback risk: if a cited benchmark is unpublished or its leaders[rank] no longer exists, the pick degrades to 'empty state'. But an authored caveat/verb/label still exist on the PickSeed; rendering an honest-empty body while keeping authored claim copy could surface a claim label ('Best for X') with no sourced model, OR (worse) keep a stale authored datedAnchor that no longer matches any board. This can hide that a previously-cited leader vanished, presenting absence as if it were authored honest-empty.
violations.lockedDecisionViolated: No fabricated/unsourced leader; honest-empty reality must be real, not a silent degradation that masks a broken citation.
violations.fix: Distinguish authored-empty (empty=true in the seed, intentional) from resolve-failure-empty (a non-empty pick whose (slug,rank) failed to resolve). The latter must NOT silently render as honest-empty content; it should drop the pick entirely and surface an admin-visible warning (and fail seed-use-cases --verify, which the synthesis already requires for leaderRank existence). Only seed-authored empty=true picks may render the judge-it-yourself body.

violations.location: useCasePage.sections BOTTOM LINE populated example + right rail 'dated stamp (newest pick date)'
violations.issue: The Bottom Line resolves/asserts a single leader sentence ('claude-fable-5 leads EQ-Bench Creative Writing'), and the rail prints one 'As of MON YYYY' computed as the NEWEST pick date across the use case's picks. Computing 'newest date across picks' is a min/max reduction over multiple benchmarks' dates rendered as one page-level stamp; combined with a single-leader Bottom Line it can read as a use-case-level 'current best' verdict synthesized from several boards, which is the soft composite the page is meant to avoid.
violations.lockedDecisionViolated: No cross-benchmark synthesized standing; the bottom line must cite ONE named benchmark + date + caveat, not a use-case-level rollup.
violations.fix: Bind the populated Bottom Line to exactly one benchmark and its own date (the primary board it names), not a max-across-picks stamp. If the rail shows a date, make it the date OF the specific board named in the bottom line, labeled with that board's name, so it cannot read as a use-case-wide 'as of'. Never compute a page-level date by reducing over multiple boards.

trustIntegrity.summary: The synthesis is strong on the headline locked decisions: there is genuinely no invented composite/overall score, no model catalog (no price/speed/latency/context cards), no unified mega-table, no score bars, and the resolve-from-leaders hybrid plus required-caveat field correctly keep every populated pick welded to one benchmark, one date, one caveat while never forcing scaffold or self-reported scores onto the PR1 trust spine. The honest-empty and self-reported realities are designed as full-weight content, which is the right instinct. However the audit found five subtler places where forbidden patterns can re-enter, in descending severity: (1) the explorer col-3 readout 'most boards live' vs 'no live board' and the answerable/sparse grouping ARE a per-use-case rollup of trust tiers across the backing benchmarks, a soft cross-benchmark verdict the locked decisions forbid; fix by deriving the label from a single authored honestEmpty flag or each board's own status, never a live-board tally. (2) The EQ-Bench 'share the top of' near-tie pick risks one PickStamp asserting two models where only one is welded/sourced, or a 2-row mini-table inside a pick; fix by emitting one full welded ScoreStamp per cited co-leader. (3) SCAFFOLD reusing the gold tile collapses chromatically with Contested/Disputed and risks a scaffold score reading as a bare-model score; fix by making the what-was-tested line a REQUIRED seeder-enforced field so the word, not the color, carries it. (4) getUseCaseDetail's silent drop-to-empty on a failed (slug,rank) resolve can mask a broken citation as authored honest-empty; fix by separating authored empty=true from resolve-failure and failing verify. (5) The page-level 'newest pick date' rail stamp plus a single-leader Bottom Line can read as a use-case-wide synthesized 'current best'; fix by binding the bottom line and its date to one named board, not a max-across-picks reduction. None of these are fatal to the design, but each must be resolved in spec before PR2b/PR2c or the page will quietly reintroduce the cross-benchmark comparison the page exists to flag. Zero em or en dashes in this output.
